title: USD/JPY pinned below 160.00 into Fed decision and Tokyo CPI release
body: USD/JPY traded in a tight 80-pip range on Tuesday, closing near 159.62 after the session touched a low of 158.96 in early Asia and a high of 159.79 late in the New York session.

title: NZD/USD slips below 0.59 as US Dollar firms into Fed week
body: NZD/USD slipped 0.4% on Tuesday, closing near 0.5885 after a session high close to 0.5925 and a fresh rejection from the 0.5900 handle.

title: AUD/USD holds steady ahead of CPI and the Fed
body: AUD/USD posted a near-flat close on Tuesday, settling around 0.7180 after a session that traded within a roughly 70-pip range between 0.7130 and 0.7200.

title: USD/IDR: Recovery potential after geopolitical de-escalation – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong notes USD/IDR has turned lower with the broader US Dollar (USD) pullback, but says recent Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) softness reflects external uncertainty from a potential prolonged United States (US)–Iran conflict and vulnerability to energy shocks.

title: China: Growth projected to moderate – DBS
body: DBS Group Research economists Byron Lam and Daisy Sharma highlight that China’s 1Q 2026 real Gross Domestic Product (GDP) rose to 5.0% year-on-year from 4.5% in Q4 2025, supported by strong external demand but uneven domestic momentum.

title: Gold: Inflation shock weighs on haven metal – TD Securities
body: TD Securities’ Head of Commodity Strategy, Bart Melek, argues that Gold is under pressure as Oil-driven inflation keeps real rates elevated and raises the opportunity cost of holding the metal.

title: USD/SGD: Range trade with defensive profile – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong describe USD/SGD as slipping on broad USD weakness and expects two‑way, range‑bound trading in the near term, with resistance around 1.2780–1.2850 and support near 1.2720–1.2670.

title: WTI Oil extends gains as prolonged Hormuz closure reinforces supply shock
body: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil trades around $98.00 on Tuesday at the time of writing, up 3.21% on the day, reaching its highest level since mid-April.

title: Canada: Fiscal update seen modestly improving deficit – TD Securities
body: TD Securities analysts expect Canada’s Spring Economic Update to show a CAD 60 billion deficit over 2026–27, slightly better than the prior budget shortfall.

title: Silver Price Forecast: Bearish momentum builds as XAG/USD struggles below SMAs
body: Silver (XAG/USD) trades with a downside bias on Tuesday, down over 2.5%, as a higher-for-longer interest rate outlook continues to weigh on price action.

title: UK: Labour slack seen containing inflation – Standard Chartered
body: Standard Chartered strategists Christopher Graham and John Davies highlight that growing labour market slack and fragile domestic demand should limit second-round inflation effects in the United Kingdom (UK).

title: NZD/USD weakens as US-Iran tensions spur risk aversion, support Dollar
body: NZD/USD trades around 0.5890 on Tuesday, down 0.35% on the day, after failing to hold above the 0.5900 level. The pair moves lower as the US Dollar (USD) strengthens, supported by increased demand for safe-haven assets.

title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bullish potential intact as traders await Fed/BoE policy meetings
body: The GBP/USD pair extends the previous day's retracement slide from the 1.3575 area, or over a one-week top, and attracts some follow-through selling on Tuesday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD looks to Fed verdict for next big break amid stalled US-Iran talks
body: Gold is trading listlessly near the $4,700 level in Asian trading on Tuesday, maintaining its familiar range, as focus now shifts toward the two-day Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy meeting starting later in the day.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD under modest pressure ahead of central banks, war clarity
body: Spot Gold trades with a soft tone on Monday, still stuck to familiar levels. The precious metal changes hands in the $4,680 price zone in the American session, after peaking at around $4,730 earlier in the day.

title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bears retain control amid Iran jitters, ahead of BoC/Fed
body: The USD/CAD pair is seen extending its retracement from the 1.3715 resistance, or over a one-week set on Friday, and drifting lower for the second consecutive day.

title: AI could quickly become the Fed’s new problem
body: A strong economy and a flimsy labor market don’t typically go hand in hand. For the Fed and other central banks, the playbook has long been straightforward: weak hiring calls for lower interest rates, while an overheating economy and inflation above target justify tighter policy.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD extends range play around $4,700, kicking off a Big week
body: Gold finds fresh demand to retake the $4,700 level in Asia on Monday, as the US Dollar pauses its advance amid a recovery in risk sentiment and ahead of the key Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy decision due later this week.

title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War and central banks in the limelight
body: The EUR/USD pair ended a three-week winning streak and settled below the 1.1700 mark as the US Dollar (USD) strengthened amid Middle East uncertainty.

title: US Dollar Weekly: Will Kevin Warsh please President Trump?
body: United States President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, faced senators, and there was no subtlety.

title: Fresh oil shock as UAE exits Saudi-controlled cartels
body: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has said it is to leave the OPEC and OPEC+ cartels of oil-producing nations, potentially threatening greater turbulence for global energy markets ahead.

title: Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens
body: An 89-year-old man has been arrested after shootings in Athens left several people wounded.

title: Cartel leader tipped to succeed 'El Mencho' captured
body: Mexico's army has captured a cartel leader tipped to succeed notorious drug lord "El Mencho".

title: Players who cover mouths in confrontations to be sent off at World Cup
body: Players who cover their mouths in confrontations with opponents to conceal discriminatory conduct face being sent off at the World Cup - after football's rule-makers voted through plans first revealed by Sky News.

title: At least 14 dead in Indonesia train crash
body: At least 14 people have been killed in a train collision in western Indonesia, with another 84 injured, according to the country's train operator.
